steps.title: Recognize vertex form with the given maximum
steps.content:
The problem specifies that the object reaches its maximum height of 70.56 feet at t = 2.1 seconds. This is the vertex of the parabola. In vertex form, f(t) = a(t - h)^2 + k, the vertex is located at (h, k).

Substituting our vertex coordinates h = 2.1 and k = 70.56:
$$f(t) = a(t - 2.1)^2 + 70.56$$
Check which choices match this h-value:
  • Choices A and D: $(t - 2.1)^2$ — correct vertex t-coordinate
  • Choices B and C: $(t + 2.1)^2$ — incorrect (vertex would be at t = -2.1)
Choices B and C are eliminated.

steps.title: Determine the sign of the leading coefficient
steps.content:
A projectile launched upward from ground level rises to a peak, then falls back down. This motion traces a downward-opening parabola, which requires a negative leading coefficient: a < 0.

Check the remaining choices:
  • Choice A: a = -16 (negative) ✓
  • Choice D: a = 16 (positive) ✗
Only choice A satisfies both the vertex form and the downward-opening requirement. That matches choice A — A is correct.
